events.title: Burdick Meat Locker, Your Meat's Best Friend
events.thought: I am not personally in the market for custom processing, but I respect any business that commits to Wednesdays and Thursdays.
events.photoCaption: The Burdick Meat Locker in Burdick, Kansas — a small white building with red trim advertising custom meat processing, curing, and butchering on Wednesdays and Thursdays.

events.title: Kansas Prairie Double Track, Properly Executed
events.thought: Kansas knows exactly what a road is supposed to look like.
events.photoCaption: A narrow dirt double-track road stretching through tall-grass Kansas prairie, viewed through the windshield, with a lone tree visible in the middle distance.

events.title: Ellis Town Campground, Surprisingly Full
events.thought: Gravel miles banked, tailwind noted, and the Ellis town camp was closer to capacity than expected — but it held one more.
events.photoCaption: Waldo parked at the Ellis, Kansas city campground with the habitat module raised, next to a small lake surrounded by large cottonwood trees in evening light.
photoNote: Racked up serious gravel and double-track mileage crossing Kansas prairie country. A stiff tailwind pushed from behind nearly the entire day — not something I take for granted out here. Optimistic that tomorrow cooperates similarly.
Rolled into Ellis town camp around 19:30 to find it nearly full. Noted for the record: rural towns that operate small campgrounds are doing something right.
